chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُعْتَكِفِ يَمْرَضُ وَ الْمُعْتَكِفَةِ تَطْمَثُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 81 – The one performing Itikaaf becomes sick, and the female performing Itikaaf menstruates

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/436)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/436)
text: الْمُعْتَكِفَةِ إِذَا طَمِثَتْ قَالَ تَرْجِعُ إِلَى بَيْتِهَا وَ إِذَا طَهُرَتْ رَجَعَتْ فَقَضَتْ مَا عَلَيْهَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, and Sahl Bin Ziyad, altogether from Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Ayoub, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the female performing Itikaaf when she menstruates. Heasws said: ‘She should return to her house, and when she is clean, she would return, and she would make up for what is upon her’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from and Sahl ibn Ziyad all from ibn Mahbub from abu Ayyub from abu Basir who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If a female in Ttikaf begins to experience Hayd (menses) she must go home; and after becoming clean return to complete Qada’ (compensatory ’Ttikaf) of what she owes.’”
